Fig. 3. Hyperparameter sensitivity on PASCAL-C. RGSE shows stable performance across a broad range of τbase, α, and σ.

Fig. 4. Qualitative results on PASCAL-C-Brit, PASCAL-C-Contrast, PASCAL-C-GaussNoise, and FoggyCityscapes (Swin-T). Green, red and blue boxes represent true positives, false negatives and false positives, respectively.

Fig. 2. Overview of Reward-Guided Semantic Evolution (RGSE). Given a test image, we first obtain the initial outputs from Grounding DINO: region proposals {vm, bm,pinit m , lm}Mm=1, and text embeddings {tk}Kk=1. To address semantic misalignment under domain shift, RGSE refines the original text embeddings through four forward-pass stages: (1) Perturbation: Gaussian noise is added to active text embeddings; (2) Reward evaluation: each perturbed embedding is scored based on cosine similarity with current and historical high-confidence visual proposals; (3) Refinement: only candidates outperforming the parent are fused via reward-weighted averaging; (4) Memory update: high-confidence proposals are added to a class-specific cache for future adaptation.
